title: fagraea obtusifolia
intro: Fagraea obtusifolia, also known as the frangipani, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries such as China, India, and Malaysia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fagraea obtusifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and can also be applied topically as a paste or ointment.
